scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text:
B

Both Alan's PR #312 and Sarah's PR #313 apply migration files that alter the events table.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. The workspace says she works Monday/Wednesday/Friday only (part-time), so she’s not available on Thursday, March 13.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out (would add ~3 weeks); the shipped release only includes JWTs and OAuth2 for Google/GitHub.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed four steps (team invite, integration connect, first task, first milestone), so "completion" now means a shorter, different process (it no longer requires the old 'first milestone' activation), making the 60%→85% comparison misleading.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace notes it was created for load testing and "never actually used for load testing" (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — All three customers were in migration batch-7, run on 2026-02-25 (listed for TICKET-1041, 1043, and 1047).

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — 1. The design summary lists two items, but pagination already has consensus (Sarah recommends cursor and Alan agrees); the webhook payload format is the only item still debated.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — src/billing/mapping.rs. The workspace notes Alan calls that migration script "the rosetta stone file" that maps plan/tier/subscription.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. Josh's customer research shows all three prospects said CSV is sufficient; no customer asked for PDF, and the Excel ask came from Sales (not the customers).

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace shows 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%) in the test distribution.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text:
B

Sarah suggested using JWTs that "feel like API keys" by issuing long-lived JWT bearer tokens — combining JWT security (rotation/revocation capability) with the simple developer experience of API keys (Mar 7, 09:00).

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The plan notes "No delivery confirmation mechanism": a customer's endpoint can return 200 without actually processing the webhook, so deliveries will appear successful and silent data-loss/reconciliation issues may only show up weeks later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The thread notes “no one has asked the customer perspective yet,” and Takeshi offered to review the proposal (available and responds within ~2 hours), so we should get his input.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace lists ~1h (hardcoded URL) + 45min (retry logic) + ~1h (generic errors) = ~2.75 hours/week; the skipped webhook test is listed as missed bugs (not quantified).

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text: C — Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519, and Sarah already owns a webhook signature module so adding another algorithm is localized and cheap (roughly a few hours / ~half day).

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even at feature 5. Calculation: without refactor = 5 days/feature × 5 = 25 days. With refactor = 3 weeks (15 days) + 2 days/feature × 5 = 15 + 10 = 25 days. Net benefit begins at feature 6.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The workspace shows this is a spec ambiguity: docs asked for "subscription start date" but engineering displayed the current billing period start (Feb 1) while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). It's a UI/label fix (change label or show both), not a backend or data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing would expand billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning) and thus land squarely in the Billing Migration work scheduled to launch March 31. That migration is already tight (ticket #847 reconciliation; slip past Mar 15 and we miss Mar 31), so this creates the main scheduling conflict.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring caught the anomaly in 12 minutes, and the root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production-scale data with historical (legacy) records (missing plan_change_date).

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined normal usage is 95 req/sec (95% of the 100 req/sec limit). With 1.5–2x traffic spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the limit and will cause 429 errors and customer-facing failures.
